implementation_summary: Correctness patch bringing aitri-reader.js and alerts/engine.js into full compliance with the Aitri ↔ Hub Integration Contract v0.1.63. Three production files modified, zero new files created, zero new npm dependencies introduced.

files_modified.description: Added ARTIFACT_MAP constant (8 phase-to-filename entries per integration contract). Added computeFileHash() private helper using node:crypto sha256. Rewrote detectDrift() with driftPhases fast path (FR-012) and correct dynamic hash check (FR-013). Updated readAitriState() to expose aitriVersion/updatedAt/createdAt (FR-010) and apply correct defaults: artifactsDir='' not 'spec', projectName=path.basename(projectDir) (FR-011). detectDrift() now accepts projectDir as second argument.

Added getInstalledAitriVersion() with module-level cache — calls execFileSync('aitri', ['--version']) once per process lifetime, returns null on any error. Added Rule 7 (VERSION_MISMATCH) to evaluateAlerts(): emits warning alert when project aitriVersion != installed CLI version and both are non-null. Exported _resetVersionCache() and _setVersionCache() as test utilities.
Added ALERT_TYPE.VERSION_MISMATCH = 'version-mismatch' to the existing frozen ALERT_TYPE object.

Updated existing 'artifactsDir defaults to spec' test to expect '' (correct per contract). Added TC-011f/h/e, TC-010h/f/e, TC-012h/f/e, TC-013h/f/e/e2 covering all new acceptance criteria. Added crypto import for sha256 computation in test setup.

Added import of _resetVersionCache, _setVersionCache test utilities. Added TC-014h/f/e/e2/e3 covering VERSION_MISMATCH alert generation, null aitriVersion, CLI unavailable, matching versions, and cache behavior.

files_created.description: This implementation manifest documenting the feature delivery.

breaking_changes.description: readAitriState() now returns artifactsDir: '' (empty string) when the field is absent or empty in .aitri, instead of the previous default of 'spec'. Callers that relied on the implicit 'spec' default when adopting a project without an explicit artifactsDir field will now receive ''.
breaking_changes.migration: No migration needed — collector/index.js uses aitriState?.artifactsDir ?? 'spec' with nullish coalescing (??) which does NOT fall back on empty string. The empty string is intentional: it causes artifact lookups to resolve to the project root, which is correct for adopted projects. Projects with explicit artifactsDir: 'spec' are unaffected.
affected_callers: lib/collector/index.js line 111 — already handles '' correctly via path.join semantics
